commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=46af0a7d0858afebe8a407a40cab911eb2dde615)
commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the explicit-image-understanding guard from `runCapability`'s native-vision skip and updates vision-skip tests to use text-only active models for configured image-model fallback coverage.

PR surface: Source -21, Tests +4. Total -17 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level. The linked issue and current `runCapability` path show how `agents.defaults.imageModel` bypasses native-vision skip, and this PR's new regression is visible from the early return before explicit model resolution.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review and contributor action are needed because the patch changes an existing config contract and the supplied proof does not demonstrate the real media path.

target.title: fix(media-understanding): skip image understanding when primary model supports vision, regardless of imageModel config (fixes #91084)
target.body:
## Summary

Remove the `hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig` guard from the vision-skip logic in `runCapability`. When the primary model supports vision natively, image understanding is now skipped unconditionally, matching the documented behavior that `agents.defaults.imageModel` is only for non-vision models.

**Changes**: 2 files, +13 −26 lines
- `src/media-understanding/runner.ts`: remove the `!hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig(...)` guard condition; remove the now-dead `hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig` function
- `src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts`: update 3 tests to use a text-only active model so they correctly verify image-model fallback behavior for non-vision primary models

## Root Cause

The vision-skip guard in `runCapability` (runner.ts:1037–1079) checked `!hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig(...)` before deciding to skip image understanding for vision-capable models. The function returned `true` whenever `agents.defaults.imageModel` was configured, even when the primary model natively supported vision. This contradicted `docs/concepts/models.md`, which states `agents.defaults.imageModel` is "used only when the primary model can't accept images."

**Invariant violated**: vision-skip must apply when the primary model supports vision, independent of `imageModel` config.

**Code path**: `runCapability` → vision-skip guard → `!hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig(...)` → `resolveImageModelFromAgentDefaults` returns entries → guard bypassed.

**Sibling audit**: no other module contains a similar vision-skip decision; the pattern is exclusive to `src/media-understanding/runner.ts`.

**Why this is the root fix**: the config-driven guard was the sole reason the skip was bypassed. Removing it restores the documented invariant without affecting the MiniMax path (preserved via `!isMinimaxVlmProvider`) or the fallback path (preserved in `resolveAutoEntries`).

## Verification

```
pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts  # 15 passed
pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.entries.guards.test.ts  # 3 passed
```

All 18 regression tests pass. `pnpm build` exits 0. `pnpm exec oxlint` reports no issues.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior or issue addressed**: Removed the `hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig` condition from the vision-skip guard so that setting `agents.defaults.imageModel` no longer forces image understanding when the primary model supports vision. The guard now skips image understanding whenever the primary model is vision-capable, matching the documented semantics ("imageModel is used only when the primary model can't accept images").

**Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 20.04 x86_64, Linux 4.19.112, Node v22.22.0, pnpm 10.25.0, OpenClaw 2026.6.2 @ d801bb5be0

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```
pnpm build && pnpm exec oxlint src/media-understanding/runner.ts && \
pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts src/media-understanding/runner.entries.guards.test.ts && \
pnpm openclaw --version
```

**Evidence after fix**:

Build succeeds with the patched runner:

```
$ pnpm openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (d801bb5)
```

Vision-skip regression suite — all 15 tests pass, including 3 tests updated to use text-only active model for fallback coverage:

```
$ pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  15 passed (15)
```

Entry guard regression suite:

```
$ pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.entries.guards.test.ts
 RUN  v4.1.7 /home/0668001395/openclawProject1
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  3 passed (3)
```

Source-level change — vision-skip guard at `src/media-understanding/runner.ts:1037–1043`, condition simplified from 4 checks to 3:

```diff
   if (
     capability === "image" &&
     activeProvider &&
-    !isMinimaxVlmProvider(activeProvider) &&
-    !hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig({
-      cfg,
-      config,
-      agentId: params.agentId,
-    })
+    !isMinimaxVlmProvider(activeProvider)
   )
```

```json
{
  "behaviorAddressed": "Vision-skip guard no longer requires absence of explicit image understanding config; it skips image understanding whenever the primary model supports vision natively, matching documented imageModel semantics",
  "assertions": [
    { "path": "src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts", "behavior": "skips image understanding when the active model supports vision (15 tests)" },
    { "path": "src/media-understanding/runner.entries.guards.test.ts", "behavior": "entry resolution guards unchanged (3 tests)" }
  ],
  "observedResult": "pnpm build exited 0, oxlint 0 warnings, pnpm test 18/18 passed, pnpm openclaw --version produces 2026.6.2",
  "testSummary": "2 test files, 18 tests passed"
}
```

**Observed result after fix**: `pnpm build` exits 0, `pnpm exec oxlint` reports 0 warnings, all 18 vision-skip and entries-guards tests pass, `pnpm openclaw --version` shows 2026.6.2.

**What was not tested**: End-to-end gateway behavior with real MiniMax provider (requires API key); gateway-level integration with `imageModel` config + vision-capable model not in test mock catalog; real Feishu/Discord channel image delivery.

## Review Findings Addressed

- Autoreview: clean (no findings)
- Oxlint: clean
- Self-review: `hasExplicitImageUnderstandingConfig` was only called from the vision-skip guard; removed as dead code

## Regression Test Plan

```
pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.vision-skip.test.ts      # primary coverage
pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.entries.guards.test.ts   # entry guards
pnpm test src/media-understanding/runner.auto-audio.test.ts       # auto audio
pnpm test src/media-understanding/defaults.test.ts                # defaults
```

## Merge Risk

Low. The change removes a guard condition that contradicted documented behavior. The `agents.defaults.imageModel` config continues to work as a fallback when the primary model does NOT support vision (the `resolveAutoEntries` path at line ~741 still returns the configured imageModel). The MiniMax special-casing (`isMinimaxVlmProvider`) is preserved. No API surface changes; only internal guard logic.
target.author: zenglingbiao

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
